title: ISE GUI Admin Session Activity — Concurrent Sessions, Unusual Geo, and Long-Running Sessions

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.5 secure authentication — anomalous session detection.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Same admin account login from two IPs in different countries within 5 min. UC-17.1.72 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Admin from one location only. No fire.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Administrative-Operations-Audit* — codes `60005` (admin login), `60007` (admin logout), `60008` (admin session expired). GeoIP enrichment via `geoip(source_ip)` against MaxMind/ISO data.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=60005
| iplocation source_ip
| stats count, dc(source_ip) as src_ips, values(Country) as countries by admin_user
| where src_ips > 2 OR mvcount(countries) > 1
description: Detects unusual ISE admin session patterns — same admin account from multiple IPs, multiple countries within a short window, or sessions left running >24h.
value: An admin account simultaneously active from two countries is a strong compromise signal. Long-running sessions are forgotten browser tabs that violate session-management policy.
implementation: Schedule every 1h. Requires GeoIP database installed in Splunk.
visualization: Admin geo map; concurrent-session table; long-session gauge.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Admin transitioning between office and VPN.
(b) Multi-region admin teams with shared accounts (anti-pattern, but exists).
grandmaExplanation: If an admin account suddenly logs in from two countries in the same hour, something is wrong — they can't be in two places at once. We watch for these red flags.
